services.name: Duke University Hospital
services.notes: Top-ranked academic medical center, nationally recognized in cardiology, oncology, ophthalmology, and neurology. Level 1 trauma center.

services.name: UNC Hospitals
services.notes: Major academic medical center affiliated with UNC School of Medicine. Top-ranked in multiple specialties. NC Cancer Hospital on campus.

services.name: WakeMed Health & Hospitals
services.notes: Raleigh's largest hospital system, Level 1 trauma center, heart and vascular center, comprehensive emergency services.

services.name: CVS Pharmacy
services.notes: Full pharmacy, vaccinations, MinuteClinic walk-in, extended hours. Dozens of locations across the Triangle.

services.name: Harris Teeter
services.notes: Full supermarket with deli, bakery, pharmacy, organic section. Major Triangle grocery chain with numerous locations.

services.name: Wegmans - Raleigh
services.notes: Premium full-service supermarket with extensive prepared foods, restaurant-quality deli, organic produce, and in-store dining.

services.name: GoRaleigh Transit
services.address: GoRaleigh Station, 214 S Blount St, Raleigh, NC 27601
services.notes: Raleigh city bus system. Senior half-fare with Medicare card. Routes cover most of Raleigh metro. GoTriangle regional routes connect to Durham and Chapel Hill.

services.name: Raleigh-Durham International Airport (RDU)
services.notes: Major regional airport with direct flights to most US cities and select international destinations. Located centrally between Raleigh and Durham.

services.name: Duke Gardens - Sarah P. Duke Gardens
services.notes: 55-acre public garden on the Duke University campus. Free admission. Beautiful walking paths (dogs on leash welcome in designated areas).

services.name: NC State Veterinary Hospital
services.notes: NC State College of Veterinary Medicine teaching hospital. Full-service specialty and emergency vet care. One of the top veterinary hospitals in the Southeast.

services.name: Duke Primary Care - Raleigh
services.notes: Duke Health primary care clinic in Raleigh. Accepting new patients, MyChart portal, Medicare accepted.

services.name: NC Museum of Art
services.notes: Free state art museum with extensive permanent collection, outdoor sculpture park and trails, rotating exhibitions. 164-acre Museum Park.

services.name: Kroger family of stores
services.notes: Largest US supermarket operator; regional banners (Ralphs, Fred Meyer, King Soopers, Smith's, Fry's, etc.).

attractions.name: NC Museum of Art
attractions.description: Free state art museum with European, American, and African art collections plus a 164-acre outdoor sculpture park

attractions.name: Sarah P. Duke Gardens
attractions.description: 55-acre public garden on the Duke campus with terraced gardens, Asian garden, and native woodland walks

attractions.name: William B. Umstead State Park
attractions.description: 5,600-acre state park between Raleigh and Durham with hiking trails, fishing lakes, and mountain biking

attractions.name: NC Museum of Natural Sciences
attractions.description: Largest natural history museum in the Southeast with live animals, dinosaur exhibits, and nature research center

attractions.name: Durham Food Scene
attractions.description: Nationally recognized dining district with diverse restaurants, from James Beard-nominated chefs to authentic international cuisine
attractions.frequency: Daily, restaurants concentrated in American Tobacco Campus and downtown Durham

attractions.name: Durham Performing Arts Center (DPAC)
attractions.description: 2,700-seat performing arts venue hosting Broadway tours, concerts, comedy, and dance performances

attractions.name: State Farmers Market
attractions.description: Year-round farmers market with local produce, plants, restaurants, and seasonal events on 75 acres

attractions.name: Neuse River Greenway Trail
attractions.description: 28-mile paved trail along the Neuse River, perfect for walking, cycling, and dog walking

attractions.name: First Friday Raleigh
attractions.description: Monthly art walk in downtown Raleigh with gallery openings, live music, and food trucks

attractions.name: Historic Oakwood
attractions.description: One of Raleigh's oldest neighborhoods with 450+ preserved Victorian homes and an annual candlelight tour
attractions.frequency: Walking tours available year-round, candlelight tour in December
